mission.title: Install and Configure Kong Gateway
mission.description: Kong Gateway is a high-performance, cloud-native API gateway that provides advanced traffic management and extensibility through plugins. It is ideal for orchestrating microservices and managing API traffic, offering features like load balancing, authentication, and analytics. Key trade-offs include the complexity of setup and the need for ongoing management of configurations and plugins.

steps.title: Install the CLI
steps.description:
Install the Kong CLI tool to manage your Kong Gateway installation.

```bash

curl -Lo kong.tar.gz https://github.com/Kong/kong/releases/download/3.9.1/kong-3.9.1.linux-amd64.tar.gz

tar -xzf kong.tar.gz

sudo mv kong /usr/local/bin/

```

> Download the Kong CLI binary.
> Extract the downloaded tarball.
> Move the binary to a directory in your PATH.

steps.title: Create a Kubernetes Cluster
steps.description:
Create a new Kubernetes cluster using gcloud with the specified version and node count.

```bash

gcloud container clusters create kong-cluster --num-nodes=3 --zone=us-central1-a --cluster-version=1.30

```

> Create a cluster with 3 nodes in the specified zone.

steps.title: Set Up Kubeconfig
steps.description:
Configure kubectl to use the new cluster's credentials.

```bash

gcloud container clusters get-credentials kong-cluster --zone=us-central1-a

```

> Fetch the cluster credentials.

steps.title: Verify Cluster Nodes
steps.description:
Check that the nodes in the cluster are up and running.

```bash

kubectl get nodes

```

> List all nodes in the cluster.

steps.title: Install Kong Gateway
steps.description:
Deploy Kong Gateway using Helm with the specified version.

```bash

helm repo add kong https://charts.konghq.com

helm repo update

kubectl create namespace kong

helm install kong kong/kong --namespace kong --version 3.9.1

```

> Add the Kong Helm repository.
> Update the Helm repository to fetch the latest charts.
> Create a namespace for Kong.
> Install Kong Gateway in the 'kong' namespace.

steps.title: Post-Install Configuration
steps.description:
Set up monitoring and autoscaling for Kong Gateway.

```bash

kubectl apply -f https://raw.githubusercontent.com/Kong/kong/master/kong-ingress.yaml

kubectl autoscale deployment kong --cpu-percent=50 --min=1 --max=10 --namespace kong

```

> Apply the ingress configuration.
> Set up autoscaling for the Kong deployment.

uninstall.title: Uninstall Kong Gateway
uninstall.description:
Remove Kong Gateway and associated resources. Warning: This will delete all configurations and data.

```bash

helm uninstall kong --namespace kong

kubectl delete namespace kong

```

> Uninstall the Kong release.
> Delete the Kong namespace and all resources within it.

upgrade.title: Upgrade Kong Gateway
upgrade.description:
Backup existing configurations, upgrade Kong, and verify the installation.

```bash

kubectl get all -n kong -o yaml > kong-backup.yaml

helm upgrade kong kong/kong --namespace kong --version 3.9.1

kubectl get pods -n kong

```

> Backup current configurations.
> Upgrade Kong to the specified version.
> Verify that all pods are running after the upgrade.
commands: kubectl get all -n kong -o yaml > kong-backup.yaml

troubleshooting.title: Error: 'Kong ingress controller not found'
troubleshooting.description:
**Cause:** The Kong ingress controller may not be installed or running.

**Fix:**
Run 'kubectl get pods -n kong' to check the status of the pods.

troubleshooting.title: Error: 'Failed to connect to database'
troubleshooting.description:
**Cause:** The database service may not be running or accessible.

**Fix:**
Ensure the database service is running and accessible from the Kong pods.

troubleshooting.title: Error: 'Kong Gateway is not responding'
troubleshooting.description:
**Cause:** Kong Gateway pods may be crashing or not starting.

**Fix:**
Check pod logs with 'kubectl logs <pod-name> -n kong' for errors.

troubleshooting.title: Error: 'Service not found'
troubleshooting.description:
**Cause:** The service may not be correctly defined or deployed.

**Fix:**
Verify the service configuration and ensure it is deployed in the correct namespace.

versionNotes.changes: Added support for new plugins, improved performance for traffic routing.
versionNotes.migrationSteps: Review and update any configurations using deprecated authentication methods.

resolution.summary: You should see the output of 'kubectl get pods -n kong' showing all pods in the Running state.

prerequisites.description: Ensure you have the necessary tools and resources before starting the installation.
